title: Pipeline Observability, Incident Response, Governance, and Improvement · Release Engineering Workshop
summary: A delivery pairing session predicts each automation transition before producing the first working delivery operations and governance defense for pipeline observability, incident response, governance, and improvement. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of delivery operations and governance defense while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Instrument queue, duration, critical path, cache, failure class, artifact, deployment, and recovery with bounded cardinality and privacy.
Set log, artifact, cache, attestation, test-report, and deployment-evidence retention from recovery, audit, privacy, quota, and cost needs.
Reconstruct event, workflow version, runner, inputs, dependency graph, first causal failure, retries, side effects, and downstream state before rerunning.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ci Pipeline Telemetry Cardinality Privacy
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop delivery incident exposes identifiers in logs, consumes growing storage, receives blind reruns, and retains permanent exceptions. Reconstruct first cause, contain authority, restore known artifacts, govern retention, and expire risk. Competency probe: Instrument queue, duration, critical path, cache, failure class, artifact, deployment, and recovery with bounded cardinality and privacy.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ci Pipeline Telemetry Cardinality Privacy.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for instrument queue, duration, critical path, cache, failure class, artifact, deployment, and recovery with bounded cardinality and privacy. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ci Pipeline Telemetry Cardinality Privacy, keep Ci Pipeline Telemetry Cardinality Privacy connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

Reconstruct first cause, contain authority, restore known artifacts, govern retention, and expire risk. Competency probe: Instrument queue, duration, critical path, cache, failure class, artifact, deployment, and recovery with bounded cardinality and privacy.

content.value: NIST Secure Software Development Framework — Controls risk-based preparation, software protection, secure production, vulnerability response, evidence, and continuous improvement.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement
steps.instruction:
Append the CI/CD evidence item headed "  # Evidence: ci-policy-exception-continuous-improvement-xhvgl". Tailor its delivery-operations.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run GitHub Actions, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, Google Cloud, and deployment work later in authorized disposable transfer environments.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement, keep Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-policy-exception-continuous-improvement-xhvgl
  - competency: ci-policy-exception-continuous-improvement
    artifact: delivery-operations.yaml
    decision: policy exception continuous improvement
    environment: Node 24 TypeScript monorepo with three lockfiles
    fault-injection: cancel an older run while its external mutation is in progress
    retained-proof: queue duration critical path failure class recovery and cost evidence
    case-key: xhvgl
    change: |
      metrics: queue-critical-path-failure-class-recovery
      cardinality: bounded
      privacy: actor-and-input-minimized
      retention: risk-cost-policy
      incident: contain-preserve-restore-notify
      first-causal-failure: reconstructed
      credential-response: revoke-and-rotate
      artifact-recovery: prior-verified-digest
      exception: owner-reason-expiry
      drill: quarterly
      improvement: outcome-reviewed
    verify: reconstruct one delivery incident from event workflow runner dependency artifact deployment telemetry audit containment and recovery evidence
    failure: Reject unbounded metric labels, forever retention, blind reruns, workflow cancellation as containment, or non-expiring central policy exceptions.
    repair: Measure useful flow safely, retain by need, diagnose first cause, contain authority and mutations, restore verified artifacts, expire exceptions, and drill.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop delivery incident exposes identifiers in logs, consumes growing storage, receives blind reruns, and retains permanent exceptions. Reconstruct first cause, contain authority, restore known artifacts, govern retention, and expire risk. Competency probe: Govern required workflows, allowed actions, runner groups, permissions, attestations, exceptions, expiry, drills, metrics, and risk-based improvement.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement, keep Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Govern required workflows, allowed actions, runner groups, permissions, attestations, exceptions, expiry, drills, metrics, and risk-based improvement.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement, name the event, revision, actor, permission, runner, dependency, artifact, environment, deployment, and recovery boundary, Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Workshop Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement, trace this concrete input and predict evaluation, shell, job graph, cache, artifact, trust, deployment, signal, rollback, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A workshop delivery incident exposes identifiers in logs, consumes growing storage, receives blind reruns, and retains permanent exceptions. Reconstruct first cause, contain authority, restore known artifacts, govern retention, and expire risk. Competency probe: Govern required workflows, allowed actions, runner groups, permissions, attestations, exceptions, expiry, drills, metrics, and risk-based improvement.

For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement, challenge the documented misconception with workflow, run, artifact, policy, fault, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: A centrally mandated pipeline template can replace local ownership and learning.
